title: Stop The Operator Station From The Main Screen
summary: Stop the operator station using the documented CYCLE STOP control on the operator station main screen.
when_to_use: Use when the operator needs to stop the operator station from the operator station main screen using the documented HMI control.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for system shutdown from the system API screen.
Do not use this runbook as a lockout/tagout procedure.
Do not use this runbook for fault recovery, manual reset, jogging, or axis referencing procedures.

responsibilities: Access the operator station HMI main screen.
Press CYCLE STOP to stop the operator station.

safety_notes: This runbook only covers the documented HMI stop action for the operator station.
The related lockout/tagout reference indicates additional steps are required before LOTO, including turning the operator station electrical panel power switch to OFF before LOTO.
No additional safety controls, confirmations, or recovery actions are provided in the source section for this stop action.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI main screen

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI main screen
system_preconditions: Operator station HMI is available so the operator can access the main screen and use the CYCLE STOP control.

visual_references.description: System screen artifact from page 82 associated with stopping the operator station; nearby text references using CYCLE STOP on the operator station main screen.
Operator station control panel figure showing CYCLE STOP on the primary operator HMI screen.
Operator station HMI artifact with nearby text referencing pressing CYCLE STOP to stop the automatic tipping cycle.
Operator station HMI artifact with nearby text referencing pressing CYCLE STOP before jogging.
Operator station HMI artifact with nearby text referencing pressing CYCLE STOP during fault recovery context.
Operator station HMI screen snippet with nearby text referencing CYCLE STOP in non-recoverable fault recovery context.

steps.title: Go to the operator station main screen
steps.purpose: Access the documented screen where the stop control is located.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station main screen.
steps.expected_result: The operator station main screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The main operator station screen is visible and available for interaction.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access or display the operator station main screen.

actions.label: Open operator station main screen
actions.exact_action: Go to the operator station main screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the page 82 stopping/startup screen artifact associated with the stopping the operator station procedure.
Use the operator station control panel figure as a visual reference for the main operator HMI screen and its controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator station main screen cannot be accessed or displayed.

steps.title: Locate the CYCLE STOP control
steps.purpose: Identify the documented HMI control used to stop the operator station.
steps.instruction: Locate the CYCLE STOP control on the operator station main screen.
steps.expected_result: The CYCLE STOP control is identified on the operator station main screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The CYCLE STOP control is visible on the operator station main screen.
steps.failure_condition: The CYCLE STOP control cannot be found on the displayed operator station main screen.

actions.label: Find CYCLE STOP
actions.exact_action: Locate the CYCLE STOP control on the operator station main screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the operator station stopping/startup screen context associated with CYCLE STOP.
Look for the CYCLE STOP control on the operator station control panel figure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The CYCLE STOP control is not visible on the operator station main screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Stopping the operator station: Press CYCLE STOP on the operator station main screen.

Nearby text on page 82 includes using CYCLE STOP on the operator station main screen to stop the operator station.

steps.title: Press CYCLE STOP
steps.purpose: Execute the documented stop action for the operator station.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is stopped using the documented main screen control.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station stop action is carried out using CYCLE STOP on the operator station main screen.
steps.failure_condition: The source does not provide confirmation behavior, recovery steps, or escalation details if pressing CYCLE STOP does not stop the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the figure to identify the CYCLE STOP control before pressing it.
Use the page 82 artifact associated with the stopping procedure as supporting visual context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Pressing CYCLE STOP does not stop the operator station.
Additional recovery, confirmation, or escalation details are needed beyond what the source provides.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Stopping the operator station: Press CYCLE STOP on the operator station main screen.
Figure 4-19 includes CYCLE STOP on the operator station control panel.

success_criteria: The operator station is stopped using the documented CYCLE STOP control on the operator station main screen.
healthy_conditions: The operator station main screen is accessible.
The CYCLE STOP control is visible on the operator station main screen.
The documented stop action is performed from the operator station main screen.
failure_conditions: The operator station main screen cannot be accessed.
The CYCLE STOP control cannot be located on the operator station main screen.
Pressing CYCLE STOP does not stop the operator station.
The source does not provide additional recovery, confirmation, or escalation details if the stop action does not work.
escalation_guidance: If the operator station main screen cannot be accessed, stop and seek site-specific support because the source does not provide an alternate method in this procedure.
If the CYCLE STOP control cannot be located or does not work, stop and escalate for SME or support review because the source does not provide recovery or confirmation details.
